preview_text:
The model favours Senegal at 61%, assigning them a clear but not dominant edge. Iraq's win probability stands at 13%, roughly one-fifth of Senegal's chance. The remaining outcome space—a draw—carries meaningful weight at 27%.

The W/D/L distribution suggests a moderately competitive match. The 48-percentage-point gap between the favourite and the underdog leaves room for upset, and the draw probability at 27% indicates the model expects a reasonably tight contest where stalemate is a plausible result. Neither team is heavily favoured to dominate decisively.

The market consensus (sportsbooks and Polymarket combined) diverges noticeably from the model. Both commercial sources rate Senegal's win probability 9–11 percentage points higher, at 70–72%, and compress the draw probability to 17–19%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is more accurate; the divergence likely reflects different statistical methods and market sentiment on available information.
